course_description: This course provides students with the fundamental skills to translate business-related texts from a broad range of industries and sectors. Students will be trained in specific translation approaches and strategies to be able to operate within the translation marketplace with a broad range of commercial texts.

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
Public holidays:
Alternative arrangements for affected students will be announced through the Blackboard site.
Class allocation:
In order to optimise the student experience, it may be necessary to reallocate students to a different class from their first choice. Before this happens, every effort will be made to enable students to voluntarily change into an alternative class that is suitable. Please note: Teaching staff do not have access to the timetabling system to help with class allocation.
Therefore, should you need help with your timetable and/or allocation of classes, please ensure you email
hass.mytimetable@uq.edu.au
from your UQ student email account with the following details:
Full name,
Student ID, and
the Course Code
Additional information and support can be found
here
.
course_aims: This course develops key transaltion skills and introduces students to text types and transaltion issues specific to a business context.ﾠ

learning_outcomes.description: identify and articulate the main trends of translation in business settings, including their commonalities and respective features
acquire an understanding of the different digital tools available to translators and the use of digital platforms/media in professional practice in a business context
apply knowledge of fundamental translation theories in analysing and solving the issues involved in translating business texts
develop knowledge of ethical standards and their application to real-life situations in relation to business translation

assessment_details.title: Practical translation assessment 1 (assignment)
assessment_details.task_description: Chinese-stream students You will be required to translate one Chinese passage into English. This is an assignment that forms part of the weekly practical translation exercises. The translation assessment passage will be approximately 250 words in length and will be available on Blackboard. Japanese-stream students You will be required to translate one Japanese passage into English. This is an assignment that forms part of the weekly practical translation exercises. The translation assessment passage will be approximately 250 words in length and will be available on Blackboard. STATEMENT ON AI & MT Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit your translation via Turnitin by the due date. File name: Assessment1_TRIN7140_Surname. Each page should have the course code, course title and your name at the top (set up a template). The translation should be typed in a 12-point font, double-spaced, and presented professionally.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Practical translation assessment 2 (in class)
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Chinese-stream students You will be required to translate one Chinese passage into English in class. Working time 60 minutes + reading time 5 minutes . There will be a choice of two passages, each approximately 250 words long. The translation passages will be provided in class. Japanese-stream students You will be required to translate one Japanese passage into English in class. Working time 60 minutes + reading time 5 minutes. There will be a choice of two passages, each approximately 250 words long. The translation passages will be provided in class. While machine translation tools such as Google Translate can be useful for translators, students are not permitted to use such tools to work on this assessment item. Students can utilise such tools and develop their post-editing skills at a later stage after they have consolidated their basic translation skills and established their translation standards. STATEMENT ON AI & MT This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Practical translation assessment 4 (in class)
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Chinese-stream students You will be required to translate one English passage into Chinese in class. Working time 60 minutes + reading time 5 minutes . There will be a choice of two passages, and each passage is equivalent to approximately 250 English words long. The translation passages will be provided in class. Japanese-stream students You will be required to translate one English passage into Japanese in class. Working time 60 minutes + reading time 5 minutes . There will be a choice of two passages, and each passage is equivalent to approximately 250 English words long. The translation passages will be provided in class. While machine translation tools such as Google Translate can be useful for translators, students are not permitted to use such tools to work on this assessment item. Students can utilise such tools and develop their post-editing skills at a later stage after they have consolidated their basic translation skills and established their translation standards. STATEMENT ON AI & MT This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 3 (23 Feb - 15 Mar)
learning_activities.topic: Introduction to translating in business settings Introduction to the fundamental skills of translation in business settings Learning outcomes: L02, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 4 To Week 8 (16 Mar - 26 Apr)
learning_activities.topic: Translation from Chinese/Japanese Practical exercises in translation from Chinese/Japanese to English Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 9 To Week 13 (27 Apr - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Translation from English Practical exercises in translation from English to Chinese/Japanese Learning outcomes: L01
